name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students (Technical Degree)
summary: Scholarship support for girl students admitted to first year technical degree courses or second year technical degree courses through lateral entry in AICTE-approved institutions.
benefits: Rs. 50,000 per annum for every year of study, up to 4 years for first-year admitted degree students.
Up to 3 years of support for students admitted into second year through lateral entry.
The amount is a lump sum for college fee, computer, stationery, books, equipment, software, and related education expenses.
required_documents: Valid family income certificate issued by State/UT Government
Academic details from the qualifying examination
Institution verification through the National Scholarship Portal
For renewal, passing certificate or marksheet and letter from the Head of Institution
eligibility_text: Applicant should be a girl student.
Applicant should be admitted to first year of a degree-level technical course, or second year of a degree-level technical course through lateral entry.
Maximum two girl children per family are eligible.
Family income from all sources should not be more than Rs. 8 lakh per annum.
Applicant should not be receiving another scholarship or financial assistance from another source during the course.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The girl-student condition applies to the user.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Gender or girl-student applicability is needed because this scheme is specifically for girl students.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: This scheme is specifically for girl students.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user is currently a student.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Student status is missing.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: This scheme is for students, but the user is not currently a student.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user is at undergraduate level, matching a degree-level scholarship.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Education level is missing; this scheme is for degree-level study.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: This scheme is for degree-level technical study, but the user's education level does not appear to be undergraduate.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user's course appears to be technical or engineering-related.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Course name is missing; this scheme requires technical degree study.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: The course name does not appear to be a technical degree course.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The admission type matches first-year regular admission or second-year lateral entry.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Admission type is missing; this scheme requires first-year degree admission or second-year lateral entry.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: The admission type does not match first-year regular admission or second-year lateral entry.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user says the institution is AICTE-approved.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: AICTE approval status of the institution is missing.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: This scheme requires an AICTE-approved institution.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user's family income is within the Rs. 8 lakh annual limit.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Annual family income is missing.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: The user's family income is above the Rs. 8 lakh annual limit.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user says they have a valid income certificate.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Income certificate availability is unknown.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: A valid State/UT income certificate is needed for application readiness.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The number of girl children in the family is within the scheme limit.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Number of girl children in the family is missing.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: The scheme allows a maximum of two girl children per family.

eligibility_rules.matched_reason: The user says they are not receiving another scholarship or financial assistance.
eligibility_rules.missing_message: Other scholarship or financial assistance status is missing.
eligibility_rules.failed_reason: The scheme does not allow receiving another scholarship or financial assistance during the course.

application_steps: Go to the National Scholarship Portal.
Complete registration or login using the official NSP flow.
Select the AICTE Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students (Technical Degree), if available for the current academic year.
Fill academic, institution, income, and other required details only on the official portal.
Submit the application before the student deadline.
Track institute and nodal verification status on the official portal.

application_window.notes: Application window captured from NSP All Scholarships listing. Verify again on NSP before advising a real user.

name: AICTE Saksham Scholarship Scheme for Specially Abled Student (Technical Degree)
summary: Scholarship support for specially-abled students pursuing technical degree education in AICTE-approved institutions.
benefits: Rs. 50,000 per annum for every year of study, up to 4 years for first-year admitted degree students.
Up to 3 years of support for students admitted into second year through lateral entry.
The amount is a lump sum for college fee, computer, stationery, books, equipment, software, and related education expenses.
required_documents: Valid family income certificate issued by State/UT Government
Disability certificate or disability details as required by the official portal
Academic details from the qualifying examination
Institution verification through the National Scholarship Portal
For renewal, passing certificate or marksheet and letter from the Head of Institution
eligibility_text: Applicant should be admitted to first year of a degree-level technical course, or second year of a degree-level technical course through lateral entry.
Applicant should be a specially-abled student with disability not less than 40%.
Family income from all sources should not be more than Rs. 8 lakh per annum.
Applicant should not be receiving another scholarship or financial assistance from another source during the course.
